questions.q: Chandrayaan-1 was which country's first mission to the Moon?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-1 was India's first dedicated lunar mission.

questions.q: Which space agency built and launched Chandrayaan-1?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-1 was developed and launched by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO).

questions.q: In which year was Chandrayaan-1 launched?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-1 was launched in October 2008.

questions.q: What celestial body did Chandrayaan-1 explore?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-1 was a lunar mission that orbited the Moon.

questions.q: What major discovery is Chandrayaan-1 best known for?
options: Discovering a new moon of Earth
Detecting volcanoes erupting on the Moon
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-1's instruments confirmed the presence of water on the Moon.

questions.q: What does the word 'Chandrayaan' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Chandrayaan' is derived from Sanskrit, meaning 'Moon craft' or 'Moon vehicle'.

questions.q: Chandrayaan-1 was primarily what kind of spacecraft?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-1 was an uncrewed orbiter that circled the Moon.

questions.q: Which launch vehicle carried Chandrayaan-1 into space?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-1 was launched aboard ISRO's Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV-XL).

questions.q: Chandrayaan-1 carried a small probe that was deliberately crashed onto the Moon. What was it called?
questions.explanation: The Moon Impact Probe (MIP) was released and impacted the lunar surface.

questions.q: Did Chandrayaan-1 carry astronauts?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-1 was an uncrewed robotic spacecraft with no astronauts aboard.

questions.q: From which spaceport was Chandrayaan-1 launched?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-1 lifted off from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre at Sriharikota.

questions.q: Which NASA instrument aboard Chandrayaan-1 detected hydroxyl and water molecules on the Moon?
questions.explanation: NASA's Moon Mineralogy Mapper (M3) detected absorption features indicating water and hydroxyl.

questions.q: The Moon Impact Probe carried which national symbol painted on its sides?
questions.explanation: The Moon Impact Probe bore the Indian national flag, symbolically placing India's flag on the Moon.

questions.q: Who was the Chairman of ISRO when Chandrayaan-1 was launched in 2008?
questions.explanation: G. Madhavan Nair was the ISRO Chairman during the Chandrayaan-1 mission.

questions.q: Roughly how many scientific instruments (payloads) did Chandrayaan-1 carry?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-1 carried 11 scientific payloads from India and international partners.

questions.q: Chandrayaan-1 was placed into what kind of lunar orbit for its primary observations?
options: A highly elliptical capture orbit only
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-1 operated in a polar orbit around the Moon, enabling near-global mapping.

questions.q: Besides ISRO and NASA, agencies from which region also contributed instruments to Chandrayaan-1?
questions.explanation: The European Space Agency (ESA) and Bulgaria contributed payloads alongside ISRO and NASA.

questions.q: Approximately how long did Chandrayaan-1 remain operational before contact was lost?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-1 operated for roughly 10 months (launched Oct 2008, contact lost Aug 2009).

questions.q: Why did the Chandrayaan-1 mission end earlier than planned?
options: It ran out of fuel during launch
Radio contact was lost with the spacecraft
It was destroyed by a meteor
questions.explanation: ISRO lost radio communication with the orbiter in August 2009, ending the mission.

questions.q: In which crater region did the Moon Impact Probe target its descent?
options: On the far side near Tycho
Near the Moon's south pole (Shackleton area)
questions.explanation: The Moon Impact Probe descended near the lunar south pole, close to Shackleton crater.

questions.q: The data confirming water on the Moon from Chandrayaan-1 was published in which year?
questions.explanation: The findings on lunar water and hydroxyl were announced and published in 2009.

questions.q: Chandrayaan-1 helped pave the way for which follow-up Indian lunar mission?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-1's success led directly to the Chandrayaan-2 mission with an orbiter, lander and rover.

questions.q: On what date was Chandrayaan-1 launched?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-1 was launched on 22 October 2008 aboard a PSLV-XL.

questions.q: Which Chandrayaan-1 instrument was a Synthetic Aperture Radar provided by NASA to look for ice in polar craters?
questions.explanation: Mini-SAR (Miniature Synthetic Aperture Radar) was NASA's radar instrument probing permanently shadowed polar craters.

questions.q: What was the approximate altitude of Chandrayaan-1's planned circular mapping orbit above the Moon?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-1's nominal mapping orbit was a roughly 100 km circular polar orbit around the Moon.

questions.q: Years after contact was lost, the still-orbiting Chandrayaan-1 spacecraft was relocated in 2016 using radar by which agency?
questions.explanation: In 2016 NASA used ground-based radar (transmitting from Goldstone, with Green Bank and Arecibo receiving) to relocate the dormant Chandrayaan-1 in lunar orbit.

questions.q: Which Chandrayaan-1 X-ray instrument was contributed by the European Space Agency to map the Moon's surface chemistry?
questions.explanation: C1XS, the Chandrayaan-1 X-ray Spectrometer, was an ESA-contributed instrument mapping elemental abundances.

questions.q: What was the approximate launch mass of the Chandrayaan-1 spacecraft?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-1 had a launch mass of roughly 1,380 kg.

questions.q: The Indian-built Terrain Mapping Camera (TMC) on Chandrayaan-1 was designed mainly to do what?
options: Produce a 3D map of the lunar surface
questions.explanation: The Terrain Mapping Camera captured stereo imagery to build a three-dimensional atlas of the Moon's surface.

questions.q: Chandrayaan-1's polar mapping orbit was later raised to about what altitude after instrument overheating concerns?
questions.explanation: Due to thermal issues, Chandrayaan-1's orbit was raised from about 100 km to about 200 km in 2009.
